sections.paragraph:
τέσσαρες (Hom. et al., pseudepigr., apolog.) neut. τέσσαρα or τέσσερα (the latter is practically never found in ins and pap [Mayser p. 57 w. lit.; Rdm.2 p. 43], but is predom. in LXX-mss.). Gen. τεσσάρων (ApcEsdr 3:6 p. 27, 12 Tdf.; ApcMos 33; EpArist; Jos., Vi. 75.—τὸ διὰ τεσσάρων as a musical expr.: Dionys. Hal., Comp. Verb. 11). Dat. τέσσαρσιν (JosAs 24:15; Ac 11:5 D τέτρασιν; B-D-F §63, 1). Acc. masc. τέσσαρας (τέσσαρες as acc. [pap, LXX; for both s. Thackeray 148f] is poorly attested for the NT [B-D-F §46, 2; Mlt-H. 130; 170]) four Mt 24:31 (ἄνεμος 2); Mk 2:3 pers.; 13:27 (ἄνεμος 2); Lk 2:37; J 11:17; 19:23; Ac 10:11 al. (six times in all); Rv 4:4ab al. (29 times); Hv 1, 4, 1 al. (11 times). αἱ τ. (παρθένοι) Hs 9, 2, 3; D 10:5 (ἄνεμος 2). On τέσσερα ζῷα Rv 4:6 cp. Lohmeyer ad loc.; FDölger, Die Sonne der Gerechtigkeit u. der Schwarze 1918.—DELG. M-M. TW.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pseudepigr. pseudepigr. = pseudepigraphon, pseudepigrapha

apolog. apolog. = apologist(s)

neut. neut. = neuter (used when “n.” would be ambiguous)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Mayser Mayser = EM., Grammatik der griechischen Papyri aus der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Rdm. Rdm. = LRadermacher, Neutestamentliche Grammatik—List 6

predom. predom. = predominant(ly)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

ApcMos ApcMos = Apocalypse of Moses—List 2

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

expr. expr. = expression

Comp Comp date uncertain—List 5

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

masc. masc. = masculine

acc. acc. = accusative

Thackeray Thackeray = HT., A Grammar of the OT in Greek according to the Septuagint—List 6

NT NT = New Testament

Mlt-H. Mlt-H. = JMoulton/WHoward, A Grammar of NT Greek II: Accidence and Word-formation—List 6

pers. pers. = person(s)

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Hv Hv = Visions

Hs Hs = Similitudes

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1000
